title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our authentic, easy-to-follow recipe. Get creamy, rich, and flavorful results at home!
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant bowl of classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and a swirl of cream.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy, and authentic flavors of our Classic Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a beloved Indian dish renowned for its rich, creamy tomato gravy and succulent pieces of tandoor-cooked chicken. This classic recipe captures the authentic flavors, blending sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy notes into a truly comforting meal. It is perfect for a special dinner or a delightful family feast, promising a restaurant-quality experience right in your own kitchen.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 min

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, lemon juice, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until browned and slightly charred, resembling tandoor-cooked chicken. The chicken does not need to be fully cooked through at this stage. Set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt another 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and green cardamom pods. Sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add the finely chopped onion and cook until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste and cook for 1 minute more until aromatic.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: Add the canned crushed tomatoes, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, and remaining 1/2 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder. Stir in the soaked cashews and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have broken down and the sauce thickens.

instructions.title: Blend and Strain the Gravy
instructions.text: Remove the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods if you prefer a smoother texture. Carefully transfer the sauce to a blender and blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky gravy, pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan. This step is optional but highly recommended for authentic Murgh Makhani texture.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the pan to medium-low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces to the smooth gravy. Stir in the remaining 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, crushed kasoori methi, sugar, and the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter. Simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to finish cooking. Finally, stir in the heavy cream and heat through gently without boiling. Taste and adjust salt and sugar if needed. Garnish generously with fresh cilantro.

tips: For truly authentic flavor, consider grilling or pan-frying the marinated chicken until it gets slightly charred, mimicking a tandoor oven. This adds depth before it goes into the gravy.
Soaking cashews before blending is crucial for achieving a smooth, creamy gravy without any grainy texture. Dont skip this step for a restaurant-quality finish.

Always finish the Butter Chicken with a swirl of fresh cream and a sprinkle of crushed kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves) just before serving. These ingredients are essential for the classic aroma and richness.
article: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
